title: Execute parsed commands and format replies
description:
Implement command execution against the persistence layer.

Scope:
- Apply parsed commands to the store.
- Produce user-facing WhatsApp reply text for add, list, remove, clear, and help.
- Use one shared list.

Acceptance criteria:
- list replies include all entries in creation order with numeric positions usable by remove <number>.
- empty list replies communicate the empty state clearly.
- remove replies distinguish success from not found.
- help reply lists all supported commands from PLAN.md.
- Plain text add confirms the added entry or otherwise produces a useful reply.

Implementation notes: Keep transport-specific response sending out of this layer.
close_reason: Implemented command execution and reply formatting

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This is the command behavior boundary: apply parsed commands to the store and produce reply text that WhatsApp can send unchanged.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed by BoldForest after completing SQLite persistence epic. Implementing command execution/reply formatting in src/commands.rs only, using existing parser and StoreHandle APIs; avoiding WhatsApp transport files.

title: Add slash list and clear commands
close_reason: Added slash list and clear commands and ignored default local database

title: Define entry model and ordering semantics
description:
Define the list entry domain model and creation-order listing semantics.

Scope:
- Represent an entry with id, text, and created_at.
- Define list ordering as creation order, stable and deterministic.
- Establish how displayed numeric positions map to listed entries: position 1 is the first entry returned by creation-order listing.

Acceptance criteria:
- The model keeps implementation fields private unless a plain data carrier is justified.
- Listing uses a deterministic ORDER BY that is stable when rows have close timestamps.
- Position mapping is documented in code or tests through names/assertions, not long comments.
close_reason: Defined entry model and creation-order semantics

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Creation-order semantics established here must be reused by list display, numeric removal, WhatsApp list replies, and TRMNL rendering.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed for current coordinator session. Agent Mail active as BoldForest; reserved src/store.rs for entry model and deterministic ordering semantics.

title: Implement environment-based configuration
description:
Create configuration loading from environment variables.

Required variables:
- WHATSAPP_VERIFY_TOKEN: token Meta sends during webhook verification.
- WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN: Meta Graph API bearer token.
- WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID: WhatsApp Business phone number ID.
- TRMNL_TOKEN: shared token required by TRMNL endpoints.
- PUBLIC_BASE_URL: public base URL used to return TRMNL image URLs.
- DATABASE_PATH: SQLite file path, default list.db.
- BIND_ADDR: server bind address, default 127.0.0.1:3000.

Acceptance criteria:
- Missing required variables produce typed startup errors with clear messages.
- DATABASE_PATH and BIND_ADDR use the exact defaults from PLAN.md.
- Secrets are not logged or included in error debug output.
- No fallback config files or compatibility names are added.

Implementation notes: Prefer small typed config structs/newtypes where they improve correctness.
close_reason: Implemented environment-based configuration

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Treat env var names and defaults as contractual. Required secrets should fail startup when missing; DATABASE_PATH and BIND_ADDR are the only planned defaults.

comments.author: CodexCoordinator
comments.text: Claimed for current coordinator session after completing twl-nsw. Agent Mail MCP tools remain unavailable; coordination update recorded in beads.

title: Implement Meta Graph API text reply client
description:
Implement outbound Meta Graph API reply client.

Requirements:
- Use WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN as bearer token.
- Use WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID to construct the send-message endpoint.
- Send text replies to the inbound sender.

Acceptance criteria:
- Request construction is testable without calling the real Meta API.
- Access token is never logged.
- HTTP errors are surfaced to the webhook layer in a typed or structured way.
- No fallback provider or mock-only production path is introduced.

Implementation notes: It is acceptable to define a small trait or adapter only if it makes handler tests deterministic without over-abstracting.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: The production client should send Meta text replies, while tests should assert request construction without external network calls.

comments.author: WindyHill
comments.text: Claimed by WindyHill coordinator. Context7 checked reqwest 0.12 Client POST JSON, bearer_auth, and error_for_status patterns. Delegating Meta Graph API text reply client only; avoiding BoldForest persistence scope.

title: Initialize SQLite entries table on startup
description:
Implement SQLite connection management and direct schema initialization.

Schema requirement: create one table if missing named entries with columns id, text, created_at.

Acceptance criteria:
- Opening a configured DATABASE_PATH initializes the table if it is absent.
- No migration framework or schema version table is added.
- Errors are typed or otherwise converted into the project error type without unwrap/expect in production code.
- The design is suitable for Axum shared state without unsafe code.

Implementation notes: If concurrent access requires a large refactor or interior mutability beyond straightforward synchronization, stop and request a separate coordinator-approved bead.
close_reason: Implemented SQLite entries table initialization

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This bead defines the database initialization contract. Do not introduce migrations; later schema changes should be manual per the plan.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed for current coordinator session. Context7 checked rusqlite open/create-table guidance. Agent Mail active as BoldForest; reserved persistence initialization files.

title: Add WhatsApp webhook callback test script
description: Create a small standalone script for manually testing the WhatsApp webhook verification and POST callback endpoints on a deployed server.
close_reason: Added standalone WhatsApp webhook callback test script

title: Epic: SQLite list persistence
description:
Goal: Implement the SQLite-backed single shared list.

Scope: Direct startup initialization of one entries table, creation-order listing, add/remove/clear operations, deterministic duplicate handling, and persistence tests with temporary SQLite files.

Schema: table entries(id, text, created_at). Use rusqlite directly. No migration framework.

Coordinator note: This epic intentionally models one shared list only; do not introduce users, list IDs, categories, grocery-specific vocabulary, or schema evolution machinery.
close_reason: Completed SQLite persistence implementation and temporary-file test coverage

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: The persistence contract is the source of truth for ordering and removal semantics. Command, WhatsApp, and TRMNL work should depend on these behaviors instead of redefining them.

title: Implement TRMNL display response endpoint
description:
Implement GET /api/display?token=... for TRMNL BYOS.

Behavior:
- Validate TRMNL_TOKEN.
- Return a trmnl::DisplayResponse.
- Include an image URL based on PUBLIC_BASE_URL pointing to /trmnl/list.png?token=... so the device can fetch the rendered list.

Acceptance criteria:
- Response type and JSON shape are provided by the Rust trmnl crate where practical.
- PUBLIC_BASE_URL is used exactly as the configured public base, with no guessed fallback.
- Invalid token is rejected.
- A handler test verifies a valid response.
close_reason: Implemented TRMNL display response endpoint with encoded image URL token and tests.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This endpoint should hand TRMNL a BYOS display response pointing at the PNG endpoint using PUBLIC_BASE_URL exactly as configured.

title: Epic: Final validation and handoff
description:
Goal: Prove the complete implementation is ready to hand off.

Required final checks: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features; cargo nextest run.

Coordinator note: This epic should remain blocked until all feature/test beads are done. Any discovered missing behavior should become a new prerequisite bead rather than being hidden in the final validation bead.
close_reason: All final validation child beads are closed and required checks passed

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Keep this blocked until feature beads pass. If final checks uncover defects, create specific prerequisite beads rather than expanding this one into implementation work.

title: Epic: Service foundation and configuration
description:
Goal: Establish the Rust service foundation required by the TRMNL WhatsApp List plan.

Scope: Dependencies, configuration, application state, routing skeleton, startup wiring, and service-level conventions.

Non-goals: Do not add backward compatibility layers, migration frameworks, multi-list abstractions, or fallback implementations.

Coordinator note: Children under this epic should be completed before endpoint-specific work so later beads can focus on behavior instead of scaffolding.
close_reason: Completed foundation dependencies, configuration, router skeleton, store initialization, and startup wiring

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Complete this epic first. Later coding subagents should read config/router/store interfaces from these beads rather than reopening PLAN.md.

title: Add Coolify production deployment config
close_reason: Added Nixpacks release deployment config and Coolify README settings

title: Epic: List command parsing and execution
description:
Goal: Implement the WhatsApp-facing command language independent of transport.

Commands: plain text adds an entry; list returns all entries; remove <text> removes matching text; remove <number> removes the displayed list position; clear removes all entries; help returns supported commands.

Coordinator note: Keep parsing deterministic and small. Unknown non-empty text is Add, not an error.
close_reason: Parser, executor, and command behavior tests are complete; child beads are closed

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: The parser/executor boundary should keep WhatsApp transport details out of command logic so HTTP tests stay small and deterministic.

title: Implement remove by numeric position
description:
Implement remove by displayed numeric list position.

Behavior:
- remove 1 removes the first displayed entry.
- remove 2 removes the second displayed entry, and so on.
- Positions are 1-based because WhatsApp users see human list numbering.

Acceptance criteria:
- Out-of-range positions return a not-found/no-op result distinguishable from database errors.
- Position removal uses the same creation-order listing semantics as display/list responses.
- Tests cover first, middle/second, out-of-range, and empty-list cases.
close_reason: Implemented remove-by-position persistence

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Numeric removal must use the same 1-based display order users see in list replies and TRMNL output.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed by BoldForest after twl-uom. Context7 checked rusqlite query_row OptionalExtension and DELETE execute row-count behavior for 1-based position removal. Delegating src/store.rs only.

title: Cover TRMNL endpoints with handler tests
description:
Add HTTP handler tests for TRMNL behavior.

Required cases from PLAN.md:
- TRMNL display rejects invalid token.
- TRMNL display returns a valid response.

Additional expected cases:
- list PNG rejects invalid token.
- list PNG returns image/png for valid token.
- API log accepts representative telemetry.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests use isolated temporary persistence and router/handler invocation.
- Tests do not require a physical TRMNL device.
- Tests are deterministic under cargo nextest run.
close_reason: TRMNL handler test acceptance is covered and verification passes

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: TRMNL tests should cover auth gates and response/image behavior without requiring a device or public network.

title: Epic: WhatsApp webhook integration
description:
Goal: Implement Meta WhatsApp Cloud API integration.

Endpoints: GET /webhooks/whatsapp verifies hub.verify_token and returns hub.challenge; POST /webhooks/whatsapp receives text messages, applies list commands, and replies through the Meta Graph API.

Configuration: WHATSAPP_VERIFY_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID.

Coordinator note: Target the official Meta Cloud API only. Do not add alternate providers or compatibility wrappers.
close_reason: WhatsApp webhook integration child beads are closed and existing WhatsApp handler/reply tests pass.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This epic is transport integration only. Do not add alternate providers, local fallbacks, or compatibility layers beyond the official Meta Cloud API flow described in the child beads.

comments.author: RainyPrairie
comments.text: Plan: verify all WhatsApp child beads are closed and current code includes GET verification plus POST command/reply flow; then close this epic as completed coordination bookkeeping without code edits.

title: Implement list command parser
description:
Implement command parsing for WhatsApp message text.

Required command mapping:
- Plain non-command text => add entry.
- list => list entries.
- remove milk => remove matching text milk.
- remove 2 => remove displayed entry position 2.
- clear => clear all entries.
- help => supported commands.

Acceptance criteria:
- Commands are matched case-insensitively where appropriate for user commands.
- Entry text is preserved intentionally; any trimming behavior is explicit and tested.
- remove with a positive integer becomes remove-by-position; other remove arguments become remove-by-text.
- Empty or whitespace-only messages do not create meaningless entries; behavior is explicit.

Implementation notes: Parser should not know about WhatsApp payload shapes or SQLite.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Parser output should be pure domain data. It should not parse Meta payloads, call SQLite, or format HTTP responses.

comments.author: WindyHill
comments.text: Claimed by WindyHill coordinator. Will delegate parser implementation to a coding subagent; intended write scope is command parsing module/tests and module declaration only, avoiding BoldForest's active src/store.rs persistence work.

title: Run final format, lint, and test checks
description:
Run final required checks for the complete implementation.

Commands required by PLAN.md and TESTING.md:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features
- cargo nextest run

Acceptance criteria:
- All three commands pass.
- cargo nextest run completes within the 120 second global timeout.
- Any failures discovered here are converted into specific prerequisite beads rather than bypassed.
- The final handoff identifies the commit(s) and any remaining assumptions.
close_reason: Final fmt, clippy, and nextest checks pass

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This is validation, not implementation. If checks fail, file or complete targeted prerequisite beads before closing this final bead.

title: Epic: TRMNL BYOS display integration
description:
Goal: Implement TRMNL BYOS support for displaying the current list.

Endpoints: GET /api/display?token=... returns trmnl::DisplayResponse; GET /trmnl/list.png?token=... renders an 800x480 e-ink friendly image; POST /api/log accepts device telemetry.

Configuration: TRMNL_TOKEN and PUBLIC_BASE_URL.

Screen content: title List, entry count, entries in creation order, empty state No entries, generated timestamp.
close_reason: TRMNL BYOS child beads are complete and full verification passes

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This epic owns BYOS behavior and image rendering. The image should display the real current list, not placeholder content.

title: Implement inbound WhatsApp webhook command flow
description:
Implement POST /webhooks/whatsapp command handling.

Behavior:
- Receive inbound WhatsApp webhook payloads.
- For each text message, parse and execute the list command.
- Reply via the Meta Graph API client with the command result text.
- Non-text/status payloads should not mutate the list.

Acceptance criteria:
- Plain text inbound messages add entries.
- list, remove text, remove number, clear, and help apply expected operations.
- The handler returns an HTTP response acceptable for Meta webhook delivery after processing.
- Duplicate or multiple messages in one payload are handled deterministically.
- Failures do not expose secrets in responses or logs.
close_reason: Implemented inbound WhatsApp webhook command flow

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: This bead composes parsing, command execution, and outbound reply. Do not reimplement command semantics in the handler.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: TealHill claimed after closing command behavior bead. Delegating POST /webhooks/whatsapp composition only: parse inbound Meta payload, execute existing command layer, send replies through existing WhatsApp client, keep command semantics out of HTTP handler, and preserve secret-safe responses.

title: Render the TRMNL 800x480 list PNG
description:
Implement GET /trmnl/list.png?token=... image rendering.

Rendering requirements:
- 800x480 PNG suitable for e-ink.
- Title: List.
- Entry count.
- Current entries in creation order.
- Empty state: No entries.
- Generated timestamp.

Acceptance criteria:
- Valid token returns image/png bytes.
- Invalid token is rejected.
- Layout remains legible for empty and non-empty lists.
- Long entries are handled by truncation, wrapping, or another deterministic layout rule without panics.
- Rendering reads from the same persistence ordering used by list/remove commands.
close_reason: Implemented TRMNL 800x480 PNG rendering; judge passed fmt, clippy, and nextest.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Rendering should read live entries from persistence and produce real image/png output at 800x480, including empty state and timestamp.

comments.author: NavyForest
comments.text: Plan before commit: commit the scoped src/http.rs PNG renderer and tests together with bead state for twl-hp8; no telemetry work or unrelated refactors; verification has passed via coding subagent and judge.

title: Implement TRMNL telemetry log endpoint
description:
Implement POST /api/log for TRMNL telemetry.

Behavior:
- Accept device telemetry posted by the TRMNL device.
- Do not require list mutation.
- Return a successful response for well-formed or tolerable telemetry payloads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Handler accepts JSON telemetry without panicking.
- Handler does not store telemetry unless a later explicit requirement is added.
- Handler does not expose TRMNL_TOKEN.
- Test covers a representative telemetry POST.
close_reason: Implemented minimal TRMNL telemetry log acceptance; judge passed fmt, clippy, and nextest.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Telemetry acceptance is intentionally minimal. Do not store telemetry or add analytics unless a later bead explicitly requests it.

comments.author: NavyForest
comments.text: Plan before commit: commit only the minimal src/http.rs telemetry handler/tests and bead state for twl-lh7; no telemetry storage, analytics, token response body, or unrelated endpoint changes; coding subagent and judge checks passed.

title: Fix Nixpacks runtime binary path
Add service dependencies and crate-level safety conventions
description:
Implement the initial Cargo dependency set and crate conventions for the service.

Required dependencies from the plan: Axum for HTTP routing, rusqlite for SQLite persistence, trmnl crate for BYOS display response types, an HTTP client suitable for Meta Graph API replies, serde/serde_json for request and response payloads, and a PNG/image rendering approach for the 800x480 TRMNL image. Add Tokio if required by Axum.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cargo.toml contains only dependencies needed by the planned service.
- The crate remains Rust 2024 edition.
- Crate root forbids unsafe code unless a later explicitly approved bead waives it.
- No migration framework, alternate WhatsApp provider SDK, or backward compatibility dependency is introduced.

Implementation notes: Keep this as a minimal dependency/scaffold bead; do not implement business behavior here.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Start here. This bead should make later code compile around chosen crates, but it must not solve persistence, commands, WhatsApp, or TRMNL behavior yet.

title: Parse inbound WhatsApp text webhook payloads
description:
Define inbound WhatsApp Cloud API webhook payload handling for text messages.

Scope:
- Deserialize the official Meta webhook shape enough to extract sender/message identifiers and text message bodies.
- Ignore or gracefully no-op non-text messages and delivery/status updates.
- Keep payload parsing separate from command parsing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text messages are extracted deterministically from nested entry/change/value/message payloads.
- Unsupported payload shapes do not panic.
- Tests or fixtures cover at least one valid text payload and one non-text/status payload.

Implementation notes: Do not add support for alternate WhatsApp providers.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Keep inbound payload parsing narrow to official Meta Cloud API text messages and safe no-op behavior for status or non-text payloads.

comments.author: WindyHill
comments.text: Claimed by WindyHill coordinator. Context7 checked Serde Deserialize patterns for nested JSON, optional fields, and unknown-field tolerance. Delegating payload parsing only; avoiding persistence files and BoldForest's twl-uom scope.

title: Log bind address on startup
close_reason: Printed bound address after listener startup

title: Restore required project docs and update stale architecture state

AGENTS.md requires agents to read README.md and DESIGN.md, but neither file exists at the repo root. PLAN.md contains the project plan but remains untracked. ARCHITECTURE.md still describes an earlier state where the executable only validates configuration and HTTP routing, persistence, and WhatsApp integration are not wired, while recent commits have added Axum startup, SQLite persistence operations, command parsing, WhatsApp payload parsing, and a Meta reply client. Add the required docs or align required-reading instructions, commit project-plan content in the intended location, and update ARCHITECTURE.md current-state sections so future agents do not work from stale instructions.

title: Add local WhatsApp webhook sender
close_reason: Added local WhatsApp webhook sender script

title: Implement TRMNL token authentication
description:
Implement shared TRMNL token validation.

Scope:
- Read token query parameter on TRMNL endpoints that require it.
- Compare against TRMNL_TOKEN from config.
- Reject missing or invalid tokens.

Endpoints requiring token:
- GET /api/display?token=...
- GET /trmnl/list.png?token=...

Acceptance criteria:
- Invalid or missing token receives a non-2xx response.
- Valid token allows endpoint-specific behavior to proceed.
- Token value is not logged or echoed.

Implementation notes: Keep auth logic small and shared between the two TRMNL GET endpoints.
close_reason: Implemented shared TRMNL token validation for display and image endpoints with tests.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Shared token validation belongs here so display and PNG endpoints reject consistently and do not echo secrets.

title: Implement WhatsApp webhook verification endpoint
description:
Implement GET /webhooks/whatsapp verification.

Meta behavior:
- Read hub.verify_token and hub.challenge from the query string.
- If hub.verify_token equals WHATSAPP_VERIFY_TOKEN, return hub.challenge.
- If token is wrong or missing, reject the request.

Acceptance criteria:
- Correct token succeeds with the exact challenge body expected by Meta.
- Wrong token fails with an appropriate non-2xx status.
- Handler does not reveal the configured verify token.
- Tests cover success and failure.

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Match Meta webhook verification exactly: correct token returns challenge; wrong/missing token rejects without leaking configured token.

comments.author: WindyHill
comments.text: Claimed by WindyHill coordinator after completing twl-dtr. Context7 checked Axum 0.8 Query extraction and response return patterns. Delegating HTTP verification only; will avoid persistence files and BoldForest's twl-wrx scope.

title: Init this repository as a WhatsApp bot. I want the ability to message the bot to drive a grocery list. CRUD. Probably use SQLite.
Create Axum router and shared app state skeleton
description:
Build the Axum application skeleton and shared state wiring.

Scope:
- Define shared app state containing configuration, database/store handle, and outbound WhatsApp client dependency as needed.
- Define route registration for all planned paths, with placeholder handlers only where their behavior is owned by later beads.
- Keep handler signatures testable without starting a real listener.

Planned routes:
- GET /webhooks/whatsapp
- POST /webhooks/whatsapp
- GET /api/display
- GET /trmnl/list.png
- POST /api/log

Acceptance criteria:
- App construction is separated from process startup so HTTP handler tests can instantiate the router directly.
- No endpoint silently accepts unauthenticated TRMNL requests; later endpoint beads may fill in exact behavior.
- No business logic is implemented here except what is necessary to compile.
close_reason: Implemented Axum router and shared app state skeleton

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Build testable router/state seams for later handler tests. Placeholder handlers are acceptable only where later beads own the actual behavior.

title: Cover persistence behavior with temporary SQLite tests
description:
Add persistence tests using temporary SQLite files.

Required cases from PLAN.md:
- database initializes from an empty file.
- add/list/remove/clear work.
- matching is deterministic when duplicate text exists.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests use temporary file-backed SQLite databases, not a shared developer database.
- Tests are deterministic and suitable for cargo nextest run.
- The test suite covers remove by exact text, case-insensitive text, numeric position, and clear.

Implementation notes: Keep tests close to the persistence module unless integration tests are clearly more appropriate.
close_reason: Covered persistence behavior with temporary SQLite tests

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: These tests should lock the persistence contract before command and endpoint beads depend on it. Use isolated temporary SQLite files.

title: Harden error handling and secret-safe responses
description:
Review and tighten service-wide error handling and response mapping.

Scope:
- Ensure config, persistence, command execution, WhatsApp client, and HTTP handlers expose clear typed or structured errors.
- Map errors to appropriate HTTP statuses without leaking secrets.
- Remove unwrap/expect from production code unless impossible and explicitly justified.

Acceptance criteria:
- Production code has no avoidable unwrap/expect.
- Secrets from WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_VERIFY_TOKEN, and TRMNL_TOKEN cannot appear in ordinary error responses.
- Error behavior is covered directly or indirectly by existing tests.

Implementation notes: Keep this focused; do not perform broad refactors unrelated to error handling.
close_reason: Error handling and secret-safe response acceptance verified; no code changes required

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Do this after endpoint tests expose the main error paths. Keep changes focused on error mapping and secret-safe responses.

title: Implement deterministic remove by text
description:
Implement remove by text match.

Behavior from PLAN.md:
- remove entry by exact or case-insensitive text match.
- Matching must be deterministic when duplicate text exists.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exact matches are preferred over case-insensitive matches when both are possible.
- If duplicates match, the operation removes a deterministic entry, preferably the earliest displayed matching entry.
- The result distinguishes removed, not found, and database error cases.
- Tests cover exact match, case-insensitive match, not found, and duplicate deterministic behavior.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic remove-by-text persistence

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Duplicate text handling is deliberately specified here so later command tests can rely on deterministic removal behavior.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed by BoldForest after twl-wrx. Context7 checked rusqlite query_row optional/no-rows and DELETE execute changed-row behavior. Delegating src/store.rs deterministic remove-by-text only.

title: Implement clear-all persistence operation
description:
Implement clear all entries.

Behavior:
- clear removes every row from the single shared entries table.

Acceptance criteria:
- Clear is idempotent: clearing an empty list succeeds.
- The returned result can support user-facing command replies such as count cleared if the command executor chooses to report it.
- Tests cover clear after multiple adds and clear on an empty database.
close_reason: Implemented clear-all persistence

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Keep clear-all single-list and idempotent. Do not add archive, undo, per-user, or multi-list behavior.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed by BoldForest after twl-cyx. Context7 checked rusqlite DELETE execute affected-row count behavior. Delegating src/store.rs clear-all only.

title: Toggle list entries from WhatsApp text
close_reason: Implemented WhatsApp text toggle behavior and webhook logging

title: Wire executable startup
description:
Wire process startup for the service.

Scope:
- Load configuration.
- Initialize the SQLite store at DATABASE_PATH.
- Build the Axum router.
- Bind to BIND_ADDR and serve requests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Startup fails fast on invalid config or database initialization errors.
- The service does not create schema migrations; it only performs direct startup initialization owned by the persistence epic.
- Startup path keeps testable units separate from main.

Implementation notes: This bead may depend on persistence initialization and router skeleton; keep main.rs thin.
close_reason: Wired executable startup

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Keep main.rs thin. The executable should compose config, store initialization, router construction, and listener startup after those pieces exist.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed for current coordinator session. Context7 checked Axum 0.8 listener/serve guidance. Agent Mail active as BoldForest; reserved startup wiring files.

title: Implement add and list persistence operations
description:
Implement add entry and list entries operations.

Behavior:
- add entry stores the exact text supplied by command execution after command-layer normalization decisions.
- list entries returns current entries in creation order.

Acceptance criteria:
- Empty or whitespace-only entry policy is explicit and covered by tests or command parser rules.
- Add/list operations return typed results and do not panic in production paths.
- Operation names and boundaries make it clear this is one shared list, not per-user storage.
close_reason: Implemented shared add/list persistence operations

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Add/list are the base store operations. Avoid command parsing decisions here except what is required by the persistence API contract.

comments.author: BoldForest
comments.text: Claimed by BoldForest. Context7 checked current rusqlite execute/query_map/last_insert_rowid usage. Delegating src/store.rs add/list operations only; parser files remain WindyHill's scope.

title: Document configuration and deployment prerequisites
description:
Document operator-facing configuration and setup expectations in the repository.

Content to capture from PLAN.md:
- Required Meta WhatsApp Cloud API setup: app, business phone number ID, long-lived/permanent access token, webhook verify token, public HTTPS URL.
- Required TRMNL BYOS setup: device points to /api/display and uses shared TRMNL token.
- Runtime placement: local machine, VPS, Raspberry Pi, NAS, or similar; public access required for WhatsApp webhooks; TRMNL may be LAN-only or public.
- Environment variables and defaults.

Acceptance criteria:
- Documentation is concise but sufficient to run the service without reading PLAN.md.
- Documentation does not introduce features not in the plan.
- No sample secrets are real-looking credentials.
close_reason: Documented operator setup, configuration, deployment prerequisites, and current endpoint behavior

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: Documentation should replace PLAN.md for operators: prerequisites, env vars, defaults, endpoint URLs, and setup assumptions only.

title: Target local webhook replies to configured test number
close_reason: Updated local webhook sender to use requested WhatsApp number

title: Cover command parser and executor behavior
description:
Add unit tests for command parsing and command execution reply behavior.

Required parser tests from PLAN.md:
- plain text add.
- list.
- remove <text>.
- remove <number>.
- clear.
- help.

Additional expected tests:
- command matching case-insensitivity.
- empty/whitespace input behavior.
- list numbering aligns with remove-by-position semantics.

Acceptance criteria:
- Parser tests do not require Axum or real WhatsApp payloads.
- Execution tests use a temporary or in-memory test store consistent with the persistence design.
- Tests are deterministic under cargo nextest run.
close_reason: Required command parser and executor tests are present and judge verified fmt, clippy, and nextest pass

comments.author: yutoo
comments.text: Coordinator comment: These tests should cover both parser mapping and executor-visible behavior, especially numbering consistency between list and remove <number>.

comments.author: yutoo
